publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-05-09 18:11:33 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes material related to beekeeping, his membership in the Empire State Honey Producers' Association, and Morse's career in the Department of Entomology.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Morse was a bee biologist in the Department of Entomology. He received his B.A. in 1950, his master's degree in 1953, and his PhD in 1955, all from Cornell.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, course material and photographs related to beekeeping, his membership in the Empire State Honey Producers' Association, and Morse's career in the Department of Entomology. Also included is a bound volume titled "Burma and Nepal programs and reports," consisting of typewritten documents and correspondence concerning projects that Morse was involved with there. Its spine title is Morse--Asian journey--1982.)

emph.ref: Series III. Empire State Honey Producers Association

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Roger Morse papers, #21-23-4122. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Comb equipment - swarm in tree with wax moth damage

Effect of light on comb construction, including negatives

Effect of light on comb construction, including negatives and memos
Carol Hansen, comb honey, wax on glass
Natural nest in open - comb construction
New York State pesticide loss survey
Old photos, including National Bee-Keepers Association
Richard Hoy - glass hives - copy of image
Isopentylacetate paper with Boch and Shearer

Package bee bulletin - duplicates and unused
Honey bee pests, predators, and diseases - used in book
Seeley - CO2 and nest size - negatives and originals
Sex attractant, feeding bees, and brood patterns
Tests of effective pesticides on honey bees - Finger Lakes Producers Coop
Varroa life cycle - Byron Alexander illustration
Venom collection - small vespa nests and swarm orientation
Venom collection and effect of light on comb construction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some photo used in August 1976 bulletin

Bee hive photos for Florida paper

Propolis on bottom board, moving screen and natural comb contruction
